content.text:
<user_query>
Thoroughly scan the original Arleif transcript export for high-judgment user windows. Source root: D:\Downloads\20260618-114047\20260618-114047\.cursor\projects\workspaces-agno\agent-transcripts. Only scan transcript JSONL files whose parent transcript folder UUID begins with c, d, e, or f. Include subagents under those parent folders only if the subagent contains user role messages; otherwise ignore subagent-only implementation logs. Use the hybrid v2.5 rubric: identify key_user_move, generic_llm_counterfactual, attribution_basis, likely label, score, portfolio_ready, and why. Return a concise list of all plausible strong_highlight or portfolio candidates, plus the count of user turns scanned and any known existing candidate IDs encountered. Do not modify files.
</user_query>

Scanning the Arleif transcript export with the hybrid v2.5 rubric. Exploring the source directory and rubric definitions first.

[REDACTED]

Listing c/d/e/f transcript folders and scanning user messages in those exports.

[REDACTED]

input.description: List transcript folders starting with c/d/e/f

content.text:
Counting user turns in eligible folders and scanning high-judgment content in key transcripts.

[REDACTED]

input.pattern: undo the fan|compile time failure|can't be saying \"if\"|tool being called by an entity|split brain|deterministic|suppression|end to end sequence|skeptical

input.command: $root = "D:\Downloads\20260618-114047\20260618-114047\.cursor\projects\workspaces-agno\agent-transcripts"; $folders = Get-ChildItem $root -Directory | Where-Object { $_.Name -match '^[cdef]' }; $total = 0; $files = @(); foreach ($f in $folders) { $main = Join-Path $f.FullName "$($f.Name).jsonl"; if (Test-Path $main) { $files += $main }; if (Test-Path (Join-Path $f.FullName 'subagents')) { Get-ChildItem (Join-Path $f.FullName 'subagents') -Filter '*.jsonl' | ForEach-Object { if (Select-String -Path $_.FullName -Pattern '"role":"user"' -Quiet) { $files += $_.FullName } } } }; foreach ($file in $files) { $c = (Select-String -Path $file -Pattern '"role":"user"' -AllMatches).Matches.Count; $total += $c }; Write-Output "folders=$($folders.Count) files=$($files.Count) user_turns=$total"
input.description: Count user turns in c/d/e/f transcript folders

input.pattern: can't be saying|if we don't know|fundamental fact
split brain|end to end|undo|fan out|compile time|philosophy|skeptical|deterministic
split brain|end to end|undo|fan out|compile time|philosophy|skeptical|deterministic|if we don't know
split brain|end to end|undo|fan out|compile time|philosophy|skeptical|deterministic|if we don't know

input.command: $root = "D:\Downloads\20260618-114047\20260618-114047\.cursor\projects\workspaces-agno\agent-transcripts"; $folders = Get-ChildItem $root -Directory | Where-Object { $_.Name -match '^[cdef]' }; foreach ($f in $folders) { $main = Join-Path $f.FullName "$($f.Name).jsonl"; $paths = @(); if (Test-Path $main) { $paths += $main }; if (Test-Path (Join-Path $f.FullName 'subagents')) { Get-ChildItem (Join-Path $f.FullName 'subagents') -Filter '*.jsonl' | ForEach-Object { if (Select-String -Path $_.FullName -Pattern '"role":"user"' -Quiet) { $paths += $_.FullName } } }; foreach ($p in $paths) { Select-String -Path $p -Pattern '"role":"user"' | ForEach-Object { $line = $_.Line; if ($line -match 'are you sure|skeptical|split brain|fan out|undo|compile time|fundamental fact|suppression|deterministic|end to end|why not make|you can''t be saying|philosophy|prerequisite|verify|prove|wrong|push back|instead of|must not|invariant|ownership|race|before designing|find that out first') { $short = if ($line.Length -gt 220) { $line.Substring(0,220) + '...' } else { $line }; Write-Output "$($f.Name)|$short" } } } }
input.description: Find judgment-pattern user messages in c/d/e/f folders

input.pattern: stop pushing|should we continue|chicken and egg|operator

input.final_summary: Scanned 217 user turns across 30 c/d/e/f transcript folders (41 JSONL files). Found 2 known existing candidates in scope (`e11ea567`, `da4ffd93`) plus 9 additional plausible strong_highlight windows; 1 portfolio-ready window in scope (`e11ea567` verify-history).

content.text:
## Scan scope

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Parent folders scanned (UUID starts with **c/d/e/f**) | **30** |
| JSONL files scanned (parent + subagents with user messages only) | **41** |
| **User turns scanned** | **217** |
| Source root | `D:\Downloads\20260618-114047\20260618-114047\.cursor\projects\workspaces-agno\agent-transcripts` |

Subagents included only when they contained `"role":"user"` (e.g. `da4ffd93` subagents, `c086a982` subagents, `d5be264c` subagents, `e9f0ad9a` subagents, `ed69f8e6` subagent, `f8e50651` subagent). Subagent-only implementation logs were skipped.

---

## Known existing candidate IDs encountered (in scope)

From `Arleif's.md`, **2 of 6** known IDs fall in this c/d/e/f slice:

| ID | Window | Prior label |
|---|---|---|
| `e11ea567-00cf-4c92-912f-631f91895502` | Verify History Before Designing | `outstanding_portfolio` |
| `da4ffd93-fdc5-4848-8014-8c9028a29415` | Lint Suppression Guardrail | `strong_highlight` |

**Not in this scan** (outside c/d/e/f prefix): `a5481311`, `07653483`, `82a3118c`.

---

## Portfolio-ready candidate (in scope)

### 1. Verify History Before Designing — `e11ea567` (~turn 178)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | "You can't be saying 'if' on a fundamental fact… If we don't know that we should not be discussing anything and find that out first." |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Unlikely alone; a generic LLM would keep conditional design branches instead of blocking on a load-bearing runtime fact. |
| **attribution_basis** | User; assistant was reasoning under an "if." |
| **likely label** | `outstanding_portfolio` |
| **score** | 8 |
| **portfolio_ready** | yes |
| **why** | Stops architecture design until Agno history behavior is verified; clear stakes, assistant delta, artifact impact (trace code next). |

---

## Plausible strong_highlight candidates (9 additional + 1 known)

### 2. Router Delegation Pollution — `e11ea567` (~turn 150)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | Concierge must not rephrase the user request or invent "expected output"; that pollutes specialist internal instructions. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Might flag verbose delegation; less likely to tie it to instruction-pollution and router/member ownership. |
| **attribution_basis** | User from debug tool-call log; assistant traced Agno `determine_input_for_members`. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 7–8 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Strong architecture/process steering; assistant supplied most of the mechanism diagnosis. |

### 3. Think Before Changing Design — `e11ea567` (~turn 170)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | "you just went and changed the design? instead of thinking about what the implication would be" — demand consequence analysis before flipping flags. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Generic LLM might ask for tradeoffs; user forced revert-first, analyze-break-scenarios discipline. |
| **attribution_basis** | User course-corrected assistant's premature code change. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 7 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Excellent process judgment; pairs with window #4 below. |

### 4. Lint Suppression Guardrail — `da4ffd93` (~turn 82) — **known**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | Reject pointless silencing; group violations; each suppression needs rationale, alternatives tried, and real fixes attempted first. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Generic LLM suppresses or adds `# noqa`; user raised review standard. |
| **attribution_basis** | User after observing Stage 2 "make linter green" behavior. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 8 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Strong engineering guardrail; localized, less rare than top architecture reversals. |

### 5. Strict Lint / Fail-Fast Philosophy — `da4ffd93` (~turn 1)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | Strict typing/linting, DI over global fetches, early failure, CI/local validate in sync — guardrails to keep LLM agents in check. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Partially generic "make it strict"; specific DI/early-failure framing is user-owned. |
| **attribution_basis** | User sets product/engineering standard before assistant audit. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 6–7 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Good direction-setting; less distinctive than suppression pushback. |

### 6. DooD Multi-Tenant Collision — `d75ff518` (~turns 139–141)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | DooD was a "grave mistake" — port/name/network collisions across parallel DevPods; reject spineless non-committal advice. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Assistant had recommended DooD; user identified platform-level multi-tenant failure mode assistant missed. |
| **attribution_basis** | User after lived Supabase collision experience. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` (borderline `mindblowing_highlight`) |
| **score** | 8 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Rare platform reversal with real stakes; long debug thread weakens publishable anatomy. |

### 7. Reject Unreliable Timeout Probe — `d75ff518` (~turn 166)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | "those timeouts are unreliable, I don't want a probe that is so unreliable" — after proving false-negative race. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Generic LLM extends timeout; user rejected the probe class and pushed deterministic preflight. |
| **attribution_basis** | User caught `DIND_FAIL` + `probe_exit=1` while daemon initialized afterward. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 7–8 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Strong reliability/process design move; led to `ssh_bootstrap.py` preflight change. |

### 8. Operator Script Boundary — `e9f0ad9a` (~turn 20)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | "When we push the newly provisioned repo, do these 3 scripts end up getting pushed? Should we continue doing that? or stop?" |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Generic LLM might trace git flow; user framed customer-repo vs operator-tooling boundary. |
| **attribution_basis** | User ownership decision; assistant supplied flow analysis. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 6–7 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Good provisioner boundary judgment; assistant-led analysis. |

### 9. Chicken-and-Egg Deploy Vars — `e9f0ad9a` (~turn 56)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | Bootstrap must set new Cloud Run env vars (or obvious dummies) so deploy doesn't hit chicken-and-egg failure. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Somewhat standard deploy hygiene; user spotted gap in scaffold. |
| **attribution_basis** | User supplied required env contract from workflow YAML. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 6 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Useful tradeoff/judgment; not rare enough for portfolio. |

### 10. No Hardcoded Pooler / One Path — `fa15b2b0` (~turns 5, 18)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | Hardcoding `aws-0-` is wrong — get URL from Supabase; reject IF/legacy variability, pick one path. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Generic LLM might accept legacy fallbacks; user demanded single source of truth. |
| **attribution_basis** | User rejected assistant's dual-path bootstrap design. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` |
| **score** | 6–7 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Principled simplification; localized infra config topic. |

### 11. Contract Slot Correction — `ee304de6` (~turn 15)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **key_user_move** | Corrected false aux-slot claim; UI maps cards to slots, not backend control. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Unlikely; requires product/UI boundary knowledge. |
| **attribution_basis** | User corrected assistant contract draft. |
| **likely label** | `strong_highlight` (lower tier) |
| **score** | 5–6 |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **why** | Good correction; narrow scope, limited artifact impact evidence. |

---

## Borderline / below_bar (not listed as candidates)

- `f8e50651` fan-out challenge (~turn 51): deviation question; assistant-owned audit.
- `d75ff518` early turns: debugging/docker logs — mostly operational.
- Most remaining ~200 turns: implement/build/push/babysit, status queries, or routine steering.

---

## Summary counts

| Category | Count in c/d/e/f scan |
|---|---|
| User turns scanned | **217** |
| Known existing candidates encountered | **2** |
| Portfolio-ready in scope | **1** (`e11ea567`) |
| Plausible `strong_highlight` (incl. known) | **11** total (**9 new** + **2 known**) |
| Plausible `mindblowing_portfolio` in scope | **0** (top mindblowing windows live under `a5481311`, `07653483`) |

**Net-new findings worth mentor review:** `d75ff518` DooD collision reversal and deterministic preflight rejection are the strongest additions not already in `Arleif's.md`. The `e11ea567` delegation/design-think-first pair (~turns 150–170) is a strong secondary cluster feeding the known verify-history window (~turn 178).
